reason: The only residual finding is a missing hint for `SystemAccountEntity`, which is an application-specific example entity class, not a Nablarch framework API. All key Nablarch framework identifiers (`DbAccessSupport`, `countByParameterizedSql`, `createReader`, `DataReader`, `SqlRow`, `ExecutionContext`, `writeLog`) are already present in the hints. Users searching for batch record-count logging guidance would query on framework terms, not on a sample domain entity. Retrieval quality is not meaningfully degraded by the absence of this hint.

findings_assessment.justification: `SystemAccountEntity` is an application-defined example entity used solely to illustrate how to bind SQL parameters. It is not a Nablarch framework class and is not a term users would search with. All framework-level identifiers that drive retrieval are already present. Omitting this class name from hints has negligible effect on whether the knowledge file is surfaced for relevant queries. Phase E did not add it — the fix agent appears to have silently skipped the change, likely judging the class as outside the Nablarch API scope — but the outcome is acceptable given the low impact.
